landmark_structure.justification: Both skip links present and first in DOM, <main> contains only page content with sidebar outside, two <nav> elements each with distinct aria-label, <section> with aria-labelledby. The <header> inside <div class='sidebar'> technically exposes a banner landmark outside <body>, but this is a negligible concern given the rest of the structure is exemplary.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> 'Projects', followed by <h2> 'All projects' as section heading, then <h3> for each project card name — no skipped levels, hierarchy accurately reflects document structure.

element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: logo as , dropdown trigger as

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is mostly disciplined: nav labels, aria-current, aria-labelledby on section, and decorative alt='' on avatar are all correct. Minor issue: the visually-hidden '(current status)' inside each badge is redundant — the status text (Active/Paused/Complete) is already plain text in the <dd> of a labelled <dt>, so the hidden annotation adds noise for screen reader users without providing new information.

list_semantics.justification: Nav items in <ul> within <nav>, project cards in <ul>, dropdown items in <ul>, and project metadata as <dl> with <dt>/<dd> pairs — all list types are semantically appropriate for their content.

content_realism.justification: Realistic project names (Website Redesign, Mobile App v2, API Integration), plausible dates with varied values, varied member counts, and a real user name. No placeholder text.

expectations_met: Sidebar navigation uses <nav> with a descriptive aria-label ('Primary')
<main> wraps only the projects content area; sidebar is outside it
Both 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation' are the first focusable elements
Both <nav> elements have distinct labels ('Primary' and 'Support and account')
Nav items are in a <ul> list
User dropdown uses <button popovertarget> with <ul popover> — Popover API, not <details>/<summary>
'Profile' and 'Preferences' are <a> links; 'Sign Out' is a <button>
Project cards are in a <ul> list
Status badges convey state as visible text, not colour alone
Project card names use <h3> — appropriate given the intermediary <h2> section heading
Secondary nav has aria-label='Support and account', distinguishing it from primary nav
Logo is an containing
additional_observations: Excellent use of <dl>/<dt>/<dd> for structured card metadata (status, date, members) — far better than bare spans or divs
tabindex='-1' on <main> is the correct pattern for skip link targets that are not natively focusable
Avatar image correctly uses alt='' (decorative) since the user name is provided in the adjacent Alex Chen
The visually-hidden '(current status)' suffix on badges is a minor redundancy; removing it would improve screen reader experience without losing any information
